steps.title: Spot the possessive and plural needs
steps.content: The blank describes photos belonging to one photographer (Ansel Adams). We need a plural form because “many of” requires multiple items, and a singular possessive form to show the photos belong to the photographer.

steps.title: Apply the possessive-and-plural rule
steps.content:
To form a possessive from a singular noun, add 's (photographer's); to form a plural, add only s without an apostrophe (photos). Choice C uses photographer's early photos, combining singular possessive with plural correctly. Choices A and B use photo's (singular possessive, not plural), while B and D use photographers without a possessive form, failing to show the photos belong to one photographer.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
